Your reasoning here... Emergency general surgery laparotomies remain associated with significant mortality, particularly in emergencies involving conditions like malignant bowel obstruction, where mortality exceeds 11% and increases with age (>70 years), sepsis, and elevated creatinine. Emergency surgery for strangulated bowel also carries a mortality rate up to 3%, confirming persistently high risks tied to patient comorbidities and delays in intervention. These data validate the earlier assertion that 30-day and one-year mortalities represent a genuine area of concern demanding focused quality improvement.
\boxed{{A}}
